In 2009, Drew ran against incumbent mayor Sebastian Giuliano and lost 52–47%. He ran again in 2011, defeating Giuliano 53-46%. Facing no major party opponent, Drew was re-elected in 2013 with over 80 percent of the vote. In 2015, he was elected to a four-year term as Middletown voters approved an increase in the mayor's term.

Bhadra Fort housed royal palaces and the beautiful Nagina Baugh and the royal Ahmed Shah's Mosque on the west side and an open area known as Maidan-Shah on the east side. It had a fortified city wall with 14 towers, eight gates and two large openings covering an area of 43 acres. The eastern wall on the river bank can still be seen. The fort complex was used as a royal court during his reign. On the eastern side of a fort, there is a triple gateway known as Teen Darwaza which was formerly an entrance to the royal square, Maidan-Shah. The road beyond Teen Darwaza leads Manek Chowk, a mercantile square. On the south side along the road, there is a congregational mosque known as Jami Masjid.

The first cases of the Omicron variant in Northern Ireland were confirmed in December 2021. The Omicron variant is believed to be the most heavily mutated and highly transmissible version of coronavirus found so far. Stormont followed Westminster in tightening travel rules, requiring pre-departure COVID tests for those arriving from abroad.

The Czech National Bank (CNB) is an example of an inflation targeting central bank in a small open economy with a recent history of economic transition and real convergence to its Western European peers. Since 2010 the CNB uses 2 percent with a +/- 1pp range around it as the inflation target. The CNB places a lot of emphasis on transparency and communication; indeed, a recent study of more than 100 central banks found the CNB to be among the four most transparent ones.

7680 × 4320, sometimes referred to as 8K UHD, is a display resolution standard with a 16:9 aspect ratio and a total of 33.2 million pixels. It has double the horizontal and vertical resolution of 4K UHD (3840 × 2160), resulting in four times as many pixels. Compared to Full HD (1920 × 1080), 8K UHD has quadruple the resolution in each dimension and sixteen times the number of pixels. 7680 × 4320 was chosen as the higher resolution format for UHDTV as defined in SMPTE ST 2036-1.

The Paul Bunyan Axe was created by the Wisconsin letter winners' organization (the National W Club) and instituted as the trophy in the series in 1948. The scores of each game are recorded on the axe's handle, which is six feet long. A new axe was created in 2000, and the original axe was donated to the College Football Hall of Fame in 2003 by the Wisconsin and Minnesota athletic departments.

Initially after retiring from driving locomotives, she engaged in patriotic education with youth. After the fall of the Soviet Union she fell into obscurity and was not celebrated in Uzbekistan until after the Karimov era. She died in Tashkent in March 2010.

Angelo Sala lectured on chemistry at Rostock until the advent of war interrupted his teaching. Johann Schupp was one of his students there. Peter Lauremberg, a professor at the University of Rostock, wrote a discussion of Sala's *synopsin aphorismorum chymiatricorum*, which was then polemically answered by an unnamed critic.

Prior to developing My Taptu, Taptu was known for its mobile search technology and the Wapedia Wikipedia app. My Taptu was a free social news aggregator app for iPhone, iPod Touch, and Android devices, with an iPad version planned. Drawing heavily on Taptu's expertise in mobile search and touch-based interfaces, My Taptu aimed to organize users' social and content streams in a scalable way. Taptu intended for users to return to the app frequently throughout the day. My Taptu differentiated itself through comprehensive customization options.

On March 15, 2019, Callahan signed a three-year, $21 million contract with the Denver Broncos, reuniting him with former Bears defensive coordinator and Broncos head coach Vic Fangio. He began training camp as the Denver Broncos’ starting right cornerback in their base packages opposite starting left cornerback Chris Harris Jr. The Broncos also named Callahan the starting slot cornerback in sub-packages.  However, he was still recovering from a foot injury sustained at the end of the previous season. At the end of the 2018 season, Callahan underwent surgery to repair his broken foot. However, his foot never fully healed and suffered complications from the screw placed in his foot. He was placed on injured reserve on November 15, 2019, without playing a snap in 2019.

Owen Thomas Jones (1878-1967) was a geologist. He joined the British Geological Survey, working in Scotland. Jones was appointed the first professor of geology in Aberystwyth. Later, he became professor of geology at the University of Manchester. Starting in 1930, Jones held a position at the University of Cambridge.

After a hiatus, Nassar returns to directing with this film. The film was shot in 2010 with his 12-year-old son, Ahbi and Malaysian actor Jamali Shadat, in the lead roles. The film was produced by Nassar's wife, Kameela, a child psychologist. Malaysian artist Aruna Bala and others play supporting roles in the film.

Welsh Kale are Welsh Romani, they are found in the Welsh-speaking parts of Northwestern Wales, and they speak Welsh Kalá. The Romani population in Wales is estimated to be around 3,000. The Roma first arrived in Wales during the 16th century.

Ghate was born in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh. He started learning his art at an early age of three in Jabalpur. He then moved to Mumbai, and learned for over twelve years under Taalyogi Pandit Suresh Talwalkar.

Diffey has worked on NBC's coverage of the Olympic Games, covering luge, skeleton, and bobsled at the 2014 Winter Games in Sochi. NBC executive Sam Flood had expressed interest in having him serve as an announcer outside of motorsports while negotiating his contract with the network, and he prepared Diffey for the Olympics by having him host the Penn Relays and the Luge World Cup. Diffey remarked of serving in the Olympics: "Never in my wildest dreams did I imagine this is where my career would take me. Just working for NBC, that alone made my life. Now going to the Olympics for NBC? I just pinch myself as if to wonder is this really happening?" He returned to the Olympics to cover rowing at the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro, and also called the same events he had in Sochi at the 2018 Winter Games in Pyongchang.

In 2011, Zauner started the Philadelphia emo band Little Big League with Ian Dykstra, Kevin O'Halloran, and Deven Craige. O'Halloran and Zauner met in classes at Bryn Mawr; the two met Craige at a Post Post show and Dykstra at a party. On April 1, 2012, the band released an eponymous EP. Fronted by Zauner, it recorded music for its debut studio album in Craige's studio, at Berk's Warehouse in Philadelphia, wrapping in January 2013. The album was released on the Tiny Engines label as These Are Good People on August 6, 2013, and the band launched a tour. These Are Good People spawned the single "My Very Own You", which was released alongside a music video published by Out of Town Films.

*Sun Sun Thatha* marked Sripriya's return to directing. The film was shot fully in Malaysia. Aruna Bala and others from Malaysia played lead roles. The film was produced by Kameela Nassar, who is a producer-turned-politician.

Anutin Charnvirakul entered politics by joining Thaksin Shinawatra's Thai Rak Thai Party, quickly rising to the party's executive committee and holding ministerial posts in Thaksin's administrations. He served as Deputy Minister of Public Health (July 2004 - October 2004 and March 2005 - September 2006) and Deputy Minister of Commerce (October 2004 - March 2005). Following the dissolution of the Thai Rak Thai Party in 2007, Anutin was banned from politics for five years due to his executive role. In November 2012, he became the leader of the Bhumjaithai Party.

When a state defaults on its sovereign debt, it effectively stops or delays payments on its obligations. This action does not erase the total debt, but it immediately halts interest payments. A default significantly damages the state's reputation among creditors (Context 3, 4, 5), restricting its future access to capital markets (Context 4). In some cases, foreign businesses may react to uncertainty over debt repayment by liquidating assets and moving abroad (Context 7). While creditors cannot seize Social Security or government assistance (Context 6), they may attempt to undermine the monetary sovereignty of the debtor state.

"Stand by You" received generally positive critical reception. Jason Scott of *Popdust* described the song as a "mood-lifting anthem," noting its message of unwavering support. Scott stated that Platten etches out a storyline of "unconditional love and friendship" within the song. He further praised Platten's vocals as "assured and comforting," highlighting the "resounding hook" of the title. Scott claimed that the narrative furthers the idea of standing by someone through thick and thin, solidifying the song's message of steadfast companionship. Jason Scott is also a freelance music journalist with bylines in Billboard and AXS.

Junior Fa's career breakout came in March 2017 when he prepared to fight Daniel Tai for the vacant New Zealand Professional Boxing Association (NZPBA) heavyweight title. The fight was held on March 18 at the ABA Stadium in Auckland. Tai, a veteran boxer who had been fighting professionally since at least 2006, entered the bout with 35 previous fights and only one knockout loss on his record. The NZPBA title had been vacant for almost 5 years, since Sonny Bill Williams last held it. Fa has admitted to not allowing his body to recover from travel home from the US before taking on Tai. Despite this, Fa relied on his confidence to beat Daniel Tai on his worst day. The fight proved to be the most difficult of Fa's career, with the judges ultimately awarding him a majority decision victory with scores of 100-90, 98-94, and 95-95.

5 civilians, 11 rebels, and 22 soldiers were killed by mid-day. Much of the soldier deaths occurred when the Syrian army attempted to storm Atarib, Aleppo. The LCC reported 33 people killed by the end of day. According to S.O.H.R, 31 soldiers were killed by the end of the day.

The team was formed in 1912 by the Coaldale Athletic Association and James H. Gildea, who would manage the team for 21 seasons, with a roster of mostly local talent. While nearby towns, such as Pottsville and Gilberton favored importing players for their teams, Gildea preferred searching his own area for players. Arguably the team's two best players were James "Blue" Bonner and Jack "Honeyboy" Evans. Both men were Coaldale natives. In 1912, Gildea combined two smaller and earlier Coaldale football teams, the Old Street Stars and the Coaldale Rosebuds. While the Big Green organization dates back to 1912, the name "Big Green" was not applied to the team until after adopting its famous green jerseys in 1917. From 1912 until 1917, the team was only known as Coaldale Football. The Big Green went on to win the Curran Cup in 1921, 1922, and 1923. The 1923 championship resulted in a 3–0 win over the Maroons.

The BBC announced the adaptation of Vikram Seth's 1993 novel, *A Suitable Boy*, into an eight-part television series. The news was disclosed in an official press release by publisher Aleph Book Company on May 5, 2017. Award-winning Welsh screenwriter Andrew Davies, known for his adaptation of *War and Peace*, was finalized to adapt the screenplay. *A Suitable Boy* was commissioned by Charlotte Moore, then Director of Content, BBC, and Piers Wenger, Controller of BBC Drama. The series notably features the BBC's first entirely non-white cast. Wenger described the adaptation as a "deliberate gamble" and expressed pleasure in collaborating with Davies to bring the stories of Lata and her family to life.

The Population Division of the UN Department of Economic and Social Affairs projects future populations based on demographic trends. Their *World Population Prospects 2019* estimates the world population, which was at 7.7 billion in 2020, will reach 9.7 billion in 2050 and potentially peak at nearly 11 billion around 2100. These estimates include 235 countries or areas.

Eureka Township was officially created in May 1858. The name "Eureka," meaning "I have found it," was suggested by A.P. Searle, referring to the settlers' discovery of a better life. From 1854, the township grew as a thriving farm community, but declined between 1870 and 1950 due to rural to urban immigration.

During the 1960s, Rich's expanded further, introducing CoffeeRich, a non-dairy frozen creamer in 1961. The company also constructed a manufacturing plant in Fort Erie, Ontario, Canada the following year. This facility is now known as Rich Products of Canada, Ltd. The acquisition of Elm Tree Baking Co., in 1969, expanded Rich's offerings to include frozen baked goods.

Christy Jenkins does not make a physical appearance in the comic books and novels based on the *Charmed* television series, but her actions continue to affect the Halliwell sisters. Billie Jenkins, manipulated by Christy and the Triad into believing the Charmed Ones were corrupt, eventually reconnects with the Halliwells and remains a friend to the sisters in the canonical comic books *Charmed: Season 9* and *Charmed: Season 10*, and is referenced in one of the novels (Context 1). In *Charmed: Season 9*, issue 16, Prue Halliwell reveals that her bond to the Charmed Ones' prophecy, even after death, restricts her sisters from achieving their true powers, which made them vulnerable to the Triad's plot with Billie and Christy (Context 3, 4).

The community was founded in 1968 by Kirtanananda Swami and Hayagriva Das, two early disciples of A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. New Vrindaban developed under the guidance of Kirtanananda Swami (honored as "Srila Bhaktipada" after March 1979), and by the mid-1970s the live-in population had grown to over 100. By the 1980s the population was more than 500.

Histamine is present in almost all foods and drinks, especially fermented products. Red wine typically contains 20-200% more histamine than white wine due to fermentation with seeds and skins. Some experts believe that individuals who react to histamine may be deficient in the enzyme diamine oxidase (DAO). A study of 16 people with wine intolerance found no difference in reactions to low- and high-histamine wines. Tannins, also found in red wine, may also have an effect similar to histamine.

Raptor is a family of rocket engines developed by SpaceX exclusively for use in Starship and Super Heavy. It is fueled by liquid oxygen and methane, burning them in a highly efficient full-flow staged combustion power cycle. It is the third engine that uses such a cycle and the first that powered a flight vehicle. The Raptor engine uses methane as the fuel of choice over other rocket propellants, for reasons including the facts that methane burns 'cleaner', produces less soot, and can be directly synthesized from carbon dioxide and water. These properties make the engine highly practical, efficient and long-lasting.

In "Trickery Treat", Paige experiences guilt over her inability to prevent the massacre of the magic community instigated by Christy. Leo is also shown as still coping with Christy's betrayal. He keeps a scorch mark left by Christy on one of the manor's walls as a reminder to Piper that the house is no longer a "danger-free zone", and that she is not invincible. Christy does not make a physical appearance in either instance.

In March 1943, Hermann Voss, an art historian, director of the Wiesbaden Gallery and former deputy director of the Kaiser Friedrich Museum in Berlin took over the Special Commission. His appointment was considered odd by some, since he was known to be an anti-Nazi with a considerable number of Jewish friends and colleagues, but Hitler was known to overlook political factors when dealing with matters of art, and Voss's knowledge of southern German artwork, as well as French and Italian painting, may have decided the matter for him. Voss was not nearly as active or energetic as Posse had been, but was still "caught squarely in the flow of loot." He was prone to send out agents rather than to travel himself to make purchases, or to make dealers bring works to him.

The Queensborough River rises on the Errinundra Plateau, approximately 11 kilometres (6.8 mi) north northwest of Combienbar, in East Gippsland, Victoria. The river flows generally north northwest and north northeast, joined by one minor tributary, before joining with the Bendoc River to form the Little Plains River approximately 7 kilometres (4.3 mi) south southwest of Craigie, north of the Black-Allan Line that forms part of the border between Victoria and New South Wales. The river descends 130 metres (430 ft) over its 19-kilometre (12 mi) course.

Copenhagen's first cadastre was launched by 1689, in which the large property at Vingårdstræde 19 was listed as No. 31 in the city's East Quarter, owned by etatsrad Elias von Hubsch. The property was later divided, and in the new cadastre of 1756, the properties were listed as No. 36, owned by vice mayor Gron's widow.

The Cleveland Cavaliers debuted new uniforms before the start of the 2010-11 NBA season, returning to the original shades of wine and gold used from 1970 to 1983. The home uniform is white with a wine-and-gold horizontal stripe trim on the collar, sleeves, waistband and pant legs. The word "CAVALIERS" is displayed across the front of the jersey in block-style wine lettering, with wine lettering for the name and number. The road uniform, introduced later, is wine-colored with "Cleveland" across the front and the player's name in gold lettering with navy blue numerals both in the front and in the back. An alternate navy blue uniform was worn between 2006 and 2010 and again between 2012 and 2017. In 2022, the Cavaliers introduced new uniforms featuring the team's refreshed brand identity. The team motto "#LetEmKnow" is stitched on the inside of the collar of the Cleveland Cavaliers' uniforms.

John C. Mankins spent 25 years at NASA Headquarters and Caltech's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL). For 10 years, he was a manager in the Office of Advanced Concepts and Technology at NASA Headquarters, leading critical studies of space solar power, highly reusable space transportation, and affordable human exploration approaches. During his tenure, Mankins conceptualized several innovative projects, including the "MagLifter" electromagnetic launch assist system and the "Solar Clipper" interplanetary transport vehicle. He also led the NASA team on "A Fresh Look at Space Solar Power" and co-chaired the International Academy of Astronautics' Study on Space Solar Power. Following the dissolution of the Advanced Concepts office, Mankins became the manager of Exploration Systems Research and Technology within the Exploration Systems Mission Directorate.

Prior to the seventh generation of video game consoles, video games were typically developed for a single console with only a few games receiving cross-platform releases across multiple consoles. This was due to the unique processing architecture of each console, making development for each a closed ecosystem and requiring additional effort to port to other systems. With the seventh generation of consoles, which saw console systems use similar processor hardware as personal computers, cross-platform development for both consoles and personal computers became much easier to achieve using standard software libraries, game engines, and scripting languages that isolate platform-specific details from the specific elements for the game itself. Such tools enabled games to be released simultaneously for multiple platforms.

Otis Redding, born in Georgia in 1941, was an influential American soul singer, songwriter, and producer. Early in his career, he initially copied Little Richard's style. Sam Cooke's live album strongly influenced him. Redding studied Cooke's recordings and explored rhythm and blues, gospel, and soul music. Most of his songs are categorized as soul. "Hard to Handle" contains elements of rock and roll.

Gareth Williams is a former professional tennis player. Born in Edinburgh, Scotland, Williams was known as a doubles specialist. He achieved success as a junior player in 1993, reaching two junior Grand Slam finals. He and partner Neville Godwin finished as runners-up at both the Australian Open and US Open junior doubles, defeating Australians Ben Ellwood and James Sekulov in the latter. Williams and Godwin also reached the quarter-finals of the ATP Tour event in Durban in 1993.

Before the 2002 coup attempt, discontent within the military was created when Chávez forced them to assist the FARC, a militant Colombian guerrilla group involved in illegal drug trade, with setting up camps in Venezuelan territories, providing ammunition to fight the Colombian government, supplying ID cards so they could move freely through Venezuela and sending members of Bolivarian Circles to their camps to receive guerilla training. The International Institute for Strategic Studies (IISS) accused Chavez's government of funding FARC's Caracas office and giving it access to intelligence services, and said that during the 2002 coup attempt that, "FARC also responded to requests from (Venezuela's intelligence service) to provide training in urban terrorism involving targeted killings and the use of explosives." Venezuelan diplomats denounced the IISS' findings saying that they had "basic inaccuracies".

K. A. Mathiazhagan was a prominent figure in Tamil Nadu politics, particularly within the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK). In 1949, C. N. Annadurai, along with K.K. Neelamegam, R. Nedunchezhiyan, N. V. Natarajan, and Mathiazhagan, split from the Dravidar Kazhagam (DK) to form the DMK. These five leaders were famously known as "Aimberum Thalaivargal" (Great Five Leaders). The DMK gained popularity through agitations, including the mummunai porattam in the 1950s. Mathiazhagan played a key role in the DMK after its formation. He was elected to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly as a DMK candidate in 1967, 1971, and 1977.

Chargois was claimed off waivers by the Los Angeles Dodgers on February 23, 2018. He split both 2018 and 2019 between the AAA Oklahoma City Dodgers and Los Angeles. With the Dodgers he was 3–4 with a 4.53 ERA in 60 games. Chargois was released by the Dodgers on December 28, 2019 to pursue an opportunity in Japan.

The initial breakthrough in Mesa/Boogie's development involved hot-rodding Fender amplifiers. The second, and arguably more significant, breakthrough was developing an extra gain stage for the guitar input. This came about when Randall Smith was building a preamplifier for Lee Michaels to drive his Crown DC-300 power amplifiers. Smith added an extra tube gain stage to the preamp, incorporating three variable gain controls at critical points in the circuit, a design now known as "cascaded." Around this new principle, Smith designed a guitar amplifier, and in 1972, the Mark I was released.

The Coaldale Big Green, considered one of the most consistently successful professional football clubs in Pennsylvania's anthracite coal region, was formed in 1912 by J.H. (James "Casey") Gildea. Gildea managed the team for 21 years, during which time Coaldale's football prowess was widely publicized. The team's roster primarily consisted of local talent, a contrast to nearby towns that favored importing players. Two of the team's best players were James "Blue" Bonner and Jack "Honeyboy" Evans, both Coaldale natives. The team originated as a combination of two smaller Coaldale football teams, Old Street and Rosebud. While the organization dates back to 1912, the name "Big Green" was not adopted until after the team began wearing its famous green jerseys in 1917. Before 1917, the team was known as the Coaldale football team. The Big Green won the coveted Curran Cup, emblematic of football supremacy in the coal region, in 1921, 1922, and 1923. In 1923, the Coaldale Big Green defeated the Pottsville Maroons 3-0.

The township is governed by a three-member board of trustees, who are elected in November of odd-numbered years to a four-year term beginning on the following January 1. Two are elected in the year after the presidential election and one is elected in the year before it. There is also an elected township fiscal officer, who serves a four-year term beginning on April 1 of the year after the election, which is held in November of the year before the presidential election. Vacancies in the fiscal officership or on the board of trustees are filled by the remaining trustees.

Angelo Sala, born in Vicenza, Italy around 1576, was the son of Bernhardino Sala, a spinner. He likely began his "chymical" studies in northern Italy around 1593. After his grandfather Angelo Sala immigrated to Geneva, Sala followed, converting to Calvinism. From 1602 to 1612, he traveled extensively, working as a physician in cities including Dresden (1602), Sondrio and Ponte (1604), Nuremberg (1606), and Geneva (1609).

Walter Gilbert Alexander II was born in 1922 in Petersburg, Virginia. He came from a family of doctors who migrated from Virginia to New Jersey. Alexander attended Orange High School in New Jersey before receiving a scholarship to Rutgers University. He was a member of ROTC, the engineering program, and the Rutgers track team. A Tuskegee Airman, he graduated from Rutgers with a degree in mechanical engineering and subsequently worked for Douglas Aircraft as a draftsman in California.

Sergio Torres was born in Mar del Plata, Argentina, on July 11, 1981. He had a trial with Boca Juniors at the age of 15. Before moving to England, Torres played for CA Banfield and in the regionalised fourth division in Argentina. While playing part-time in Argentina, he worked in his father's brick factory and was training to become a brick maker. Dissatisfied with his prospects in Argentina, Torres decided to pursue his football dream in England. In November 2003, he arrived with just £150 to his name. He had a trial lined up with Brighton & Hove Albion, but was unsuccessful.

On March 2019, Bryce Callahan signed a three-year, $21 million contract with the Denver Broncos, reuniting with former Chicago Bears defensive coordinator and new Broncos head coach Vic Fangio. Callahan, recovering from a prior foot injury, participated in the team's training camp. He began training camp on the field as the starting left cornerback. In addition to his role as starting left cornerback, Callahan was also named the nickel corner in sub-packages. However, Callahan's 2019 season ended before it began when a screw in his surgically-repaired foot broke. After stem cell treatment and further complications, Callahan was placed on injured reserve on November 16, 2019, without playing a single snap for the Broncos that season.

He served on the City of Santa Clara Library Board of Trustees, the Board of Directors for KTEH, and the Board of Fellows of Santa Clara University. The plaintiff's principal allegations were that the UK government had been negligent in failing to become sufficient as a country in the supply of blood products at an earlier point, and in delaying the implementation of heat-treatment of clotting factors in order to inactivate HIV. It was further alleged that the defendants did not respond correctly to the AIDS crisis, specifically, in not reacting with urgency to move to banning imported blood products which were derived from paid donors. Another significant ground was breach of duty as provided for in statute, for example, under the National Health Service Act 1977.

NGC 7020 has a large outer ring surrounding a bright inner hexagonal zone containing an inner ring and possibly a bar. The large outer ring is completely detached from the inner hexagonal zone of the galaxy and is dominated by numerous flocculent spiral features. The outer ring has an estimated diameter of 110,000 ly (33.6 kpc). The ring is knotty and bluer than the rest of the galaxy and shows where recent star formation is occurring in NGC 7020. The possible inner ring shows protruding features at the ends of its major axis therefore classifying it as a bar. The galaxy appears to be fairly free of ionized gas, which is not surprising for an early-type galaxy.

Mikhail Lermontov's father, Yuri, experienced a troubled marriage. Literary historian and Lermontov scholar Alexander Skabichevsky noted the lack of strong evidence detailing the couple's quarrels, suggesting Yuri grew tired of his wife's nervousness, frail health, and his mother-in-law's "despotic ways." An earlier biographer, Pavel Viskovatov, proposed that Yuri's affair with Yulia, a young lodger in their house, contributed to the discord. Viskovatov claimed Yuri's violent, erratic behavior led to stresses that hastened Maria Mikhaylovna's early demise. She developed tuberculosis and died on February 27, 1817, at the young age of 21.

David J. Weber began his teaching career at San Diego State College in 1967, where he taught Latin American and borderlands history. In 1970, he lectured at the Universidad de Costa Rica as a Fulbright Lecturer. He was promoted to full professor at San Diego State University in 1973.

Haughley Park's history is intertwined with the Sulyard family, who held the estate for roughly two centuries. While the land was originally a royal hunting ground attached to Haughley Castle, it was later granted to Charles Brandon, who at the time was master of the Kings Horse under Henry VIII. Brandon surrendered the land at some point, as Queen Mary I granted the manor and park of Haughley to Sir John Sulyard (born 1518, died 1575) in 1554. This grant was a reward for Sulyard's loyalty in supporting Mary's claim to the throne and his adherence to the Catholic Church, as he was a parliamentarian during her reign. The house on the land was built around 1620. The last male heir of the Sulyard family was Edward Sulyard (1744-1799), who married twice and left his property to his three daughters upon his death.

Acetoin, a four-carbon (4C) molecule, is an important bacterial metabolite. In modern bacterial metabolism, acetoin serves as an energy storage molecule and can be used as a substitute for glucose. Acetoin is produced from α-acetolactate via α-acetolactate decarboxylase (ALDC). During exponential growth, acetoin helps regulate intracellular pH, preventing over-acidification. Once superior carbon sources are exhausted and the culture enters stationary phase, some bacteria can utilize acetoin as an external energy store. The acetoin dehydrogenase complex catalyzes the conversion of acetoin into acetyl-CoA, a reaction analogous to that of the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex. Alternatively, in some bacteria, acetoin can be reduced to 2,3-butanediol.

KAP1, also known as TRIM28, can be phosphorylated by the Ataxia-Telangiectasia Mutated (ATM) kinase. Phosphorylation of KAP1 at S824 impairs its repressive function, promoting viral lytic susceptibility. Chloroquine activates ATM in the absence of detectable DNA damage. Chloroquine treatment renders the HCMV chromatin permissive for transcription, and this effect is augmented by tumor necrosis factor (TNF) via NF-kB activation. This combination has been proposed as a method for purging HCMV, potentially in conjunction with organ transplants. The ultimate goal of antiretroviral therapy (ART) is immune reconstitution.

Heated water is used to melt a shaft over a hundred feet deep, eventually forming a cavity beneath the surface at the point where the shaft ends. By continuously replenishing this pool of heated water, the deep pocket of melted water gradually expands, renewing the supply of fresh water available to be pumped to the surface. The process was inspired by the observation that room temperature sewage injected into the arctic surface eventually formed a pocket around 100 feet down which would not refreeze as long as more sewage was continuously injected. The lifespan of a Rodriguez well is over seven years, ending when the depth of the cavity becomes too deep for the facility to efficiently heat and recirculate water.

Acetoin is a neutral, four-carbon molecule used as an external energy store by a number of fermentative bacteria. It is produced by the decarboxylation of alpha-acetolactate, a common precursor in the biosynthesis of branched-chain amino acids. Owing to its neutral nature, production and excretion of acetoin during exponential growth prevents over-acidification of the cytoplasm and the surrounding medium that would result from accumulation of acidic metabolic products, such as acetic acid and citric acid. Once superior carbon sources are exhausted, and the culture enters stationary phase, acetoin can be used to maintain the culture density. The conversion of acetoin into acetyl-CoA is catalysed by the acetoin dehydrogenase complex, following a mechanism largely analogous to the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex; however, as acetoin is not a 2-oxoacid, it does not undergo decarboxylation by the E1 enzyme; instead, a molecule of acetaldehyde is released. In some bacteria, acetoin can also be reduced to 2,3-butanediol by acetoin reductase/2,3-butanediol dehydrogenase.

Prior to the seventh generation of video game consoles, cross-platform releases were rare. Game development typically targeted a single platform due to the unique processing architecture of each console. Before the seventh generation, each console had a different processor architecture, making cross-platform development difficult. The PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, and Wii marked a shift with more standardized hardware and robust online capabilities. This change, along with new cross-platform development tools, made it easier for game companies to deliver consistent, high-quality experiences across multiple devices. These tools improved efficiency and adaptability, paving the way for more widespread cross-platform adoption.

Artifacts recovered from underwater sites need stabilization to manage the process of removal of water and conservation. The artifact either needs to be dried carefully, or the water replaced with some inert medium (as in the case of The Mary Rose). Artifacts recovered from salt water, particularly metals and glass need be stabilized following absorption of salt or leaching of metals. In-situ conservation of underwater structures is possible, but consideration needs to be given to the dynamic nature of the site. Changes to the site during intrusive investigation or removal of artifacts may result in scouring which exposes the site to further deterioration.

Golden Miller also provided Paget with her solitary Grand National victory in 1934, the same year he won the Cheltenham Gold Cup.

The Randolph area also served as a supply point for George Washington's army during their winter encampment in nearby Jockey Hollow. Randolph Township, incorporated on November 13, 1805, was named after Hartshorne Fitz-Randolph.

Duck Game is a 2D video game that features primarily shooting and platforming mechanics. In October 2017, the Trump administration announced it would end cost-sharing reduction (CSR) payments, one of the two types of subsidies under the Affordable Care Act (ACA) designed to help low-income Americans afford healthcare. The term "proa" originates from Early Modern English "prow" or "praw". It probably entered the English language via Dutch prauw and Portuguese parau, similar to Spanish proa, meaning "bow". It is likely ultimately derived from Malay perahu meaning "boat", from the Proto-Western-Malayo-Polynesian doublets *parahu and *padaw, both meaning "sailboat". Its cognates in other Austronesian languages include Javanese prau, Sundanese parahu, Kadazan padau, Maranao padaw, Cebuano paráw, Ngadha barau, Kiribati baurua, Samoan folau, Hawaiian halau, and Māori wharau.

Several programmes in the United Kingdom aim to preserve species facing challenges in their natural habitats. The freshwater pearl mussel (*Margaritifera margaritifera*), an endangered mollusc with a lifespan exceeding 100 years, exemplifies this. These mussels, once commercially important for pearls, have suffered significant habitat loss due to pollution, habitat degradation, and illegal pearl hunting. The freshwater pearl mussel was given protected status under the Wildlife and Countryside Act in 1981 and full legal protection in 1998, making pearl fishing, selling, or buying Scottish pearls illegal since then. Despite these protections, illegal pearl harvesting persists, further endangering the species. A critical aspect of the mussel's life cycle involves its larvae attaching to the gills of salmon and brown trout; declining host fish populations in rivers exacerbate the decline in mussel populations.

In 2012, an extensive scientific survey of Cobb Seamount was led jointly by Fisheries and Oceans Canada (DFO) and the United States National Oceanographic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). The survey used, among other methods, remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) and autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) to conduct high-resolution surveys of the benthic (seafloor) community above a depth of 1200 meters (3,937 feet).

The word "proa" entered English in the 16th century, derived from the Malay word *parahu*, meaning "boat". The Spanish word *proa* refers to the bow of a ship, but is unrelated. Proto-Western-Malayo-Polynesian reconstructions suggest doublets like *baNGka? and *waNGka?, with *parahu and *padaw both meaning "sailboat". Cognates exist across Austronesian languages, including Javanese *perahu*, Kadazan *padau*, Maranao *padaw*, and Cebuano *paraw*. These terms share ancestry with Proto-Hesperonesian *padaw, also "sailboat", further illustrating the widespread use of similar terms for boats in the region.

Iowa arrived in Lincoln fresh off of an upset 20–19 victory over Minnesota, the very team that scored more on Nebraska than any other team in Cornhusker history just seven weeks prior. It looked like it might be a long day for Nebraska when Iowa received the opening kickoff and returned it for a touchdown. On Nebraska's next possession, however, the game was tied up soon enough at 6–6. The squads held each other off until after the half when Nebraska was stalled and readied for a field goal attempt. The kick was no good, but a Hawkeye offside penalty on the attempt handed Nebraska a first down, which the Cornhuskers ultimately converted into a touchdown, and Iowa never recovered from the shift in fortunes. Iowa's win streak against Nebraska was ended at three, and the Cornhuskers improved to 21–10–3 in the series.

The Mohammad Bazar block in Birbhum contains part of the Deocha Pachami Dewanganj Harinsingha coal block, the largest in India. This block has been allocated to West Bengal, Bihar, Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Sutlej Jal Vidyut Nigam Limited, with West Bengal receiving the largest share of coal at 584 million tonnes. The project is located in the south-western part of Birbhum Coalfield in the Deucha and Panchamati area adjoining the Dewanganj block. The coal block covers an area of approximately 12.3-13.7 square kilometers and has estimated reserves of 2,100 million tonnes, with indicated reserves of 40.7 billion tons. The mine would occupy over 11,222 acres, encompassing a substantial portion of tribal land.

The inspiration for Calpis stemmed from founder Kaiun Mishima's travels to Inner Mongolia. In either 1902 or 1904, Mishima encountered airag (also known as kumis), a traditional cultured milk product. He was impressed by its flavor and digestive benefits, which were attributed to lactic acid produced by lactobacilli bacteria. Upon returning to Japan, Mishima resolved to develop his own version of the beverage.

Frank Lloyd Wright designed the Robie House between 1908 and 1909 from his studio in Oak Park, Illinois. The design precedent for the Robie House was the Ferdinand F. Tomek House in Riverside, Illinois, designed by Wright in 1907-08. Frederick C. Robie, then 28 years old and the assistant manager of the Excelsior Supply Company, commissioned Wright to design the house after purchasing a plot of land at his wife's recommendation. The Excelsior Supply Company, initially founded by George T. Robie to distribute sewing machine parts and later managed by him, had expanded into bicycles and automobile parts. Lora Hieronymus Robie, a graduate of the University of Chicago in 1900, influenced the selection of the property for its proximity to the university. The 60 x 180 foot corner lot, chosen in 1908, was located in Hyde Park on the South Side of Chicago.

Stella Thomas (1906-1974) was called to the Bar of England and Wales at the Middle Temple on 10 May 1933, becoming the first West African woman to achieve this distinction. In 1934, as the only African woman present, she challenged historian Margery Perham at the Royal Society of Arts, advocating for African involvement in addressing the continent's issues. By 1935, she had returned to West Africa and registered as a barrister in Sierra Leone and Nigeria, becoming the first woman lawyer in West Africa.

A Rodriguez well, also known as a "Rodwell," melts a shaft over a hundred feet deep into the ice to create a cavity for water extraction. This process, inspired by a design developed by Army engineer Raul Rodriguez at Camp Century in Greenland during the early 1960s, forms a reservoir of fresh water beneath the surface. The supply of fresh water is renewed by melting surrounding ice within the cavity. The process was inspired by the observation that when room temperature sewage was injected into the arctic surface, a pocket formed. The pocket was observed to be over 500 feet deep. The lifespan of a well is about seven years, ending when it becomes too energy-intensive to pump water from depths exceeding 500 feet.

An uncommon finding in Pompeii was a graffito referencing Ovid's *Heroides* 4, a poem about Phaedra's love for her husband's son, Hippolytus. This literary inscription was located next to a painting depicting the Roman mythical version of Pompeii. Similar to the challenges in interpreting images found in the House of Maius Castricius, understanding such graffiti requires considering both image and text. However, archaeologists have used the *Heroides* 4 graffito to suggest that Roman citizens possessed a refined understanding of art, demonstrated by their grasp of both literary references and the painting itself.

Artifacts recovered from underwater sites require stabilization to manage water removal and ensure conservation. This can be achieved either through careful drying or by replacing the water with an inert medium, as exemplified by the *Mary Rose* shipwreck. Metals and glass recovered from saltwater environments particularly need stabilization due to salt absorption or metal leaching. In-situ conservation of underwater structures is possible, but the dynamic nature of the site must be considered. Intrusive investigation or artifact removal can cause scouring, exposing the site to further deterioration.

The South African Health Products Regulatory Authority (SAHPRA), formerly known as the Medicines Control Council (MCC), is the regulatory body overseeing medicines in South Africa. The MCC initially classified dagga as a Schedule 7 substance, implying it was subject to restrictions and control, and technically illegal unless registered by the MCC. However, the MCC published regulations in recent months (prior to 2017) providing for the use of dagga for medical reasons and expressed a desire to reclassify 'cannabinoid medication' as a Schedule 6 substance. This reclassification would allow patients to access medicinal cannabis products by prescription at a pharmacy. The Dagga Couple called for the reclassification of the drug.

There are many different collections of sayings of the Desert Fathers. The earliest writings were simply ordered by the initial letter of the Abba's name in the order of the Greek alphabet, resulting in the editors starting with Anthony the Great, Arsenius and Agathon, and concluding with Cheremon, Psenthaisius and Or. These editors were the first to use the word apophthegms (meaning: saying, maxim or aphorism), resulting in this collection being known as Apophthegmata Patrum Alphabetica (The Sayings of the Desert Fathers: The Alphabetical Collection). This collection contains about a thousand items.

NGC 7020 is a weakly barred lenticular galaxy notable for its large outer ring surrounding a bright inner hexagonal zone, which contains an inner ring and possibly a bar. The outer ring is detached from the inner hexagonal zone and is dominated morphologically by numerous flocculent spiral features. The outer ring has an estimated diameter of 34,000 light-years (10.4 kpc). The ring is knotty and blue in color index maps, indicating that recent star formation is primarily occurring there. The ring also includes faint HII regions. The galaxy is classified as (R)SA0+(r), where the (r) indicates the possible inner ring that may be a bar.

Feature search (also known as "disjunctive" or "efficient" search) is a visual search process that focuses on identifying a previously requested target amongst distractors that differ from the target by a unique visual feature such as color, shape, orientation, or size. An example of a feature search task is asking a participant to identify a white square (target) surrounded by black squares (distractors). In this type of visual search, the distractors are characterized by the same visual features. The efficiency of feature search in regards to reaction time(RT) and accuracy depends on the "pop out" effect, bottom-up processing, and parallel processing. However, the efficiency of feature search is unaffected by the number of distractors present.

Bilevel positive airway pressure (BPAP), sometimes called variable positive airway pressure (VPAP), delivers two distinct pressures: inspiratory positive airway pressure (IPAP) and a lower expiratory positive airway pressure (EPAP). The lower EPAP makes exhaling easier. Some incorrectly parallel "BPAP" with "CPAP" and "APAP." BPAP is often incorrectly referred to as "BiPAP," which is the trademarked name of a specific BPAP machine manufactured by Philips Respironics. Thus, a BiPAP machine is one type of BPAP machine.

The Bhadra Fort, constructed in 1411 AD by Sultan Ahmad Shah, the founder of Ahmedabad, was designed as a royal complex. The fort housed royal palaces, the Nagina Baugh, and Ahmed Shah's Mosque on its west side, while the east side featured an open area known as Maidan-Shah. The fortified city wall surrounding the fort had 14 towers and eight gates, encompassing an area of 43 acres. The eastern wall on the river bank is still visible. During Ahmed Shah's reign, the fort complex served as a royal court. On the eastern side is the Teen Darwaza, a triple gateway that was formerly an entrance to the royal square, Maidan-Shah. The road beyond Teen Darwaza leads to Manek Chowk, a mercantile square.

Gendebien spent some years in the Belgian Congo. On his return to Europe he teamed up with Fraikin to compete in the 1952 Liège–Rome–Liège Rally using a Jaguar Mk VII saloon car. Together with Pierre Stasse, Gendebien won the sixth running of the Tulip Rally in Zandvoort in April 1954. Their car was an Alfa Romeo 1900 TI. The Gendebien and Fraiken partnership gained the nickname "the eternal bridesmaids", owing to their number of second-place finishes, but after two previous attempts they triumphed in the Liège–Rome–Liège Rally, the Coppa d'Oro delle Dolomiti and Rally Stella Alpina in 1955, driving a Mercedes-Benz 300SL. In 1956 Olivier Gendebien and Pierre Stasse finished in third place driving a Ferrari 250 GT Europa (Nr 0373).

The Czech National Bank (CNB) provides an example of inflation targeting in a transitioning economy that has converged towards advanced economy status. The CNB has targeted inflation since 1998, gradually lowering its targets to reach 2% in 2010, consistent with many developed countries. Transparency is a key element of the CNB's accountability, and it is considered an international leader in this area, publishing complete model-based forecasts.
